title: expand_vars: \ RFC 5229 §3.1 compliance unverified
description: SCENARIO: RFC 5229 §3.1 gives the example: '\' => '' => the expansion of variable foo. The Sieve lexer (per RFC 5228 §2.3) does NOT resolve \$ as an escape sequence — it passes the literal backslash through, so Form::Str contains the 8-character sequence \, $, {, f, o, o, }. expand_vars then sees \$ and consumes the backslash, emitting a bare '$' into out, then continues to push '{', 'f', 'o', 'o', '}' literally. The result is the 6-character string '${foo}' which is NOT subjected to a second-pass variable expansion. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: If the RFC intent is that '\' should expand to the value of variable foo (as the RFC example states), the implementation is non-conformant. No test exists for this case. The behavior is not documented. File a test vector against a reference implementation (Dovecot Pigeonhole) to establish ground truth, then either fix the code or document the deviation.
